publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-12-24 23:59:06 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Materials in this collection were collected or created by Peter Dougherty (1955-2015) and comprise of appointment diaries, correspondence, project files, business records, printed ephemera, news clippings, photographs, and audio and video recordings. Appointment diaries, photographs, and ephemera include coverage of the critical period between 1981 and 1983 when Hip Hop was first crossing over from its Bronx origins to the downtown club scenes of Manhattan. Other materials document Peter's involvement with the management of Yo! MTV Raps, launched on MTV America on August 6, 1988, further popularizing Hip Hop among increasingly wide audiences. Business files contain production documentation for broadcast programs and music videos produced and/or directed by Dougherty, both independently and for MTV. Artists represented include the Beastie Boys, Jean-Michel Basquiat, Arto Lindsay, LL Cool J, Fab Five Freddy, Run-DMC, DJ Jam Master Jay, along with producers Russell Simmons and Rick Rubin. Ephemera in the collection includes flyers and handbills from downtown venues such as the Negril and the Roxy, (where Hip Hop made some of its early downtown appearances) stickers, postcards, club and event invitations and announcements, press kits, publicity stills, newsletters, zines, and such as Beastie Mania, and Run DMC Special Pocket Mag. More than 100 photographs cover both social and music and television production settings, and audio/video recordings document 1980s era Hip Hop music and culture.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Peter Dougherty was a New York producer and television executive who became the co-founder and supervising producer of "Yo! MTV Raps" in 1988.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audio/visual material restricted until digitized; any audio/visual materials in the collection, once digitized, will only be accessible by researchers on-site at Cornell until 2050 or when no longer copyright protected. Boxes 6, 7.1, and 7.2 restricted until 2035.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Peter Dougherty papers, #8148. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I. Printed materials and ephemera
Bambaataa and Tommy Boy VC promo xeroxes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "7502 post card size and booklets, orig. label"--folder label; "Hip hip sizes and cards"--folder label.

scopecontent.p: "HH mix, incl. Fun, orig. label"-- folder label.

did.unittitle: Hip Hop mixed sizes and cards (from metal boxes)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "BB and HH mix cards. All Sp. Mix unless noted orig label"-- folder label; "Old label postcards, booklets, all sizes from bigger A4-1"--folder label. Folder contains original Keith Haring prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A4-1a from metal box"-- folder label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "In A4-1"--original folder.

did.unittitle: The powerHouse Arena: Wild Style the Sampler
Tommy Boy De La Soul press kit
Run DMC are Tougher Than Leather screenplay.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dougherty had a small speaking role in this film. Screenplay by Ric Menello and Rick Rubin. Based on a story by Lyor Cohen, Bill Adler, and Ric Manello.

did.unittitle: Tougher Than Leather part of script
Subseries Ib.Articles, clippings, magazines, and publicity materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly undated photocopies of articles from the 1980s.

HH xerox press rel. Tommy Boy promo etc.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Catalogue duplicate of Rare Books ML3531 .V48 2004 +

did.unittitle: VH1 Hip Hop Honors. This is the Story / Apache remix by Prestige; This is the Beat / Apache remix by Prestige

JMB (Jean-Michael Basquiat) and hip-hop MTV releases
Beasties home vid checks and check book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes undated resume.

did.unittitle: Series III. Correspondence, address books, and appointment diaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Christmas cards and invitations to MCA's and Mike D.'s weddings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includs cards from Dr. Dre and Joseph "Run" Simmons.

18th St. abandoned subway and grafitti mural

Box B1-8 Yo! Office party and Venice Beach shoot (Kodaks)
Yo! Stills from De La Soul shoot Daytona

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly raw and edited footage produced for Yo! MTV, including raw footage from a 1989 visit to Los Angeles with the Beastie Boys by Dougherty and Dr. Dre, N.W.A interview, and interview with Fab Five Freddy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Finished show provides context for how segments were used used as pseudo "live satellite" feed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shoot in LA with Dr. Dre for location roll-ins to Yo! MTV studio show.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shoot in LA with Dr. Dre for location roll-ins to Yo! MTV studio show.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: N.W.A. raw interview with Fab Five Freddy in Richmond, VA and performance

did.unittitle: Yo! Slick Rick; Big Daddy Kane; 2 Live Crew

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slick Rick: April 15, 1989; Big Daddy Kane: February 11, 1989; 2 Live Crew: January 14, 1989

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original footage of 1987 Beastie Boys "Body Snatchers" contest culminating in MTV Spring Break program held in Daytona Beach.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beasties on private jet with Rich Rubin on way to Daytona.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Kidnap" and trip to Daytona.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beasties in hotel & hot tub with contest winner & Mike D in limo with date, ten meet Larry "Bud" Melman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beastie Boys hotel arrival with winner, greet crowded bar, party in hotel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes digital surrogates of Beastie Boys projects, including the music video "Hold It Now, Hit It," a "Together Forever" MTV tour promo with Run-DMC, both directed dy Dougherty.

did.unittitle: MTV spots for (4 versions) Together Forever Tour and Beastie Boys/Run DMC; 6.5 min BB guest VJ MTV segments' Hold It Now; Hit It music video

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Big Payback Book panel at Borders NYC. Dan Charners, Peter D., Fab 5 Freddy, Cory Robbins, Ed Lover (?) and Sway (?). Dr. Dre sub for Lover?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included are live recordings made by Dougherty of sets of well-known hip-hop DJs at NYC venuses such as Negril, Cnaceteria, and the Roxy. Also present are mixtapes from commercial recordings with custom casette labels by Dougherty.

did.unittitle: And It Pays Off Big. NYC Hip Hop

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Live recording.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Live recording.

scopecontent.p: Mixtape created by the DJ.

scopecontent.p: Side A: Hip Hop Disco Dance; Side B: Be Bop Scratch Mix

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Live recording.

did.unittitle: Hole New World: Afrika Bambaata Ritz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Live recording.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compilation of answering machine messages, including ones from all the Beastie Boys, Rick Rubin, Fab Five Freddy, Charlie Ahearn, Don Letts, Dr. Revolt, Obdewl X, John "Rotten" Lydon, and Richard Hell. Side A contains messages from Dougherty's farther Philip.

did.unittitle: When Cops Can't Cop; Jazzy Jay I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Live recording.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly CD copies of Hip Hop mixtapes in this series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of Hip Hop (and some Funk) mixtape Dougherty made for Mick Jones of the Clash.
